title: Validator CLI (CDE schema + crosswalk round-trip)
context: Adopters need a CLI tool to verify that their synthetic registry data conforms to the CDE schema and that crosswalk round-trips work. The validator must also enforce the no-real-data guard (refuse to run on any input lacking a synthetic-data attestation). This makes conformance machine-checkable without requiring adopters to understand the underlying schemas. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-fixtures-001.
objective: Build the `validate` CLI in TypeScript/ESM (`tools/validate`) that: (1) checks a synthetic dataset against the CDE JSON Schema, (2) runs the CDE-to-mCODE/OMOP/Phenopackets crosswalk round-trips and validates the results against each target schema, (3) refuses to run on any input lacking a valid synthetic-data attestation (no-real-data guard), and (4) produces actionable human-readable output identifying schema violations and round-trip mismatches.
acceptanceCriteria: `validate` checks a synthetic input file against the CDE JSON Schema generated by LinkML and reports schema violations with actionable messages.
`validate` runs the crosswalk round-trips (CDE → mCODE FHIR, OMOP CDM, Phenopackets) and validates the results against each target schema; fails on round-trip mismatches.
`validate` refuses to run on any input lacking a valid synthetic-data attestation, printing a clear 'no-real-data guard' error message.
CLI is agent-neutral: no vendor-specific logic; follows CLAUDE.md agent-neutral core rules.
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green; the CLI is invoked in CI against the fixtures from fixtures-001.
output: A `tools/validate` TypeScript/ESM CLI package committed via PR with green CI, integrated with the fixtures and crosswalk tests.
